title: Soluble and insoluble A-SYN fractionation

steps: Perform extraction and detection of Triton-soluble (T-sol) and Triton-insoluble (T-insol) alpha-synuclein as described in Stojkovska and Mazzulli 53 .

Lyse individual organoids in 1% Triton X-100 extraction buffer supplemented with 1X PIC, 50 millimolar (mM) NaF, 2 millimolar (mM) NA 3 VO 4 and 0.5 millimolar (mM) PMSF. Extraction buffer %s %s Triton X-100 1% NaCl 150 mM glycerol 10% HEPES pH 7.4 25 mM EDTA 1 mM MgCl2 1.5 mM
Homogenize samples with a pestle and incubate on a platform shaker in an ice-water slurry for 00:30:00 , followed by three freeze/thaw cycles and ultracentrifugation at 100000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .
Wash the remaining pellet in Triton X-100 extraction buffer followed by another ultracentrifugation at 100000 x g, , .
Resuspend the pellet in 2% SDS buffer containing 50 millimolar (mM) Tris, pH 7.4 and 1X PIC, boil it for 00:10:00 at 100 °C (Triton-X insoluble Fraction) and label the T-insol fraction.
Sonicate Tx-Insoluble samples for 00:10:00 at 30% power, 20C in a cup horn sonicator (Qsonica-Q700), and then boil them again for 00:10:00 at 100 °C .
Ultracentrifuge Tx-Insoluble samples at 100000 x g, 21 °C, 30 minutes .
Detect protein concentrations using a BCA assay and load 30 µg of total protein for each condition.
creator: Federico Bertoli
